notes: Artifact-based evals: no live EC2. Each prompt provides the host state as if freshly discovered; the agent must produce a deploy plan + the concrete config artifacts/commands it would apply, without disturbing neighbors.

evals.name: deploy-polyrepo-next-with-neighbors
evals.prompt:
I've got an EC2 box at ubuntu@203.0.113.10 that already runs a couple of my apps, and I want to deploy a new standalone Next.js app called `acme` onto it (single Next.js repo, fullstack — it serves its own API routes) at acme.example.com.

Here's the current state of the host from a look just now:
- containers: `blog-prod` Up (image .../blog:main-abc123, published 127.0.0.1:3001->3000); `crm-prod` Up (published 127.0.0.1:3002->3000); shared `postgres`, `mongo`, `redis` all Up.
- postgres databases: blog, crm
- caddy (systemd) serving: blog.example.com, crm.example.com
- DNS: wildcard *.example.com points at this host's IP
- /data mounted, 40% full

I do NOT want to disturb blog or crm. Note: I can't give you a live SSH session right now, so treat the state above as the authoritative discovery output. Walk me through deploying acme: give me the deploy plan and the exact config files + commands you'd apply.
evals.expected_output: A deploy plan that allocates host port 3003 (3001/3002 are taken), names the project/container acme-prod, creates database+role `acme`, defines a secret namespace for acme, adds an ADDITIVE caddy vhost for acme.example.com (no DNS change needed — wildcard), and flags this as a first deploy. A filled docker-compose.app.yml (127.0.0.1:3003:3000, joins infra_net, healthcheck), a caddy vhost block reverse_proxy 127.0.0.1:3003, and the command sequence in neighbor-safe order (db-create -> set secrets -> deploy-app -> add-vhost with validate-then-reload). Must NOT reuse 3001/3002 or edit blog/crm blocks.
assertions: Allocates host port 3003 for acme and does NOT reuse the in-use ports 3001 or 3002
Creates a dedicated per-app Postgres database and role named acme (does not reuse blog/crm DBs)
Defines a per-app secret namespace/location for acme
Adds the caddy vhost additively and reloads (validate-then-reload, not restart); does not edit blog/crm vhost blocks
Generated app compose binds 127.0.0.1:3003->3000, joins the external infra_net network, and has a /health healthcheck
Generated caddy vhost reverse-proxies acme.example.com to 127.0.0.1:3003
Notes that no DNS change is needed because the wildcard *.example.com already covers acme.example.com
Command sequence is in neighbor-safe order: create DB -> set secrets -> deploy container -> add vhost + reload
Explicitly verifies the neighbors (blog and crm) remain up after the deploy

evals.name: adopt-unknown-host-safely
evals.prompt: I have an EC2 instance (i-0abc123def456, ap-southeast-1) that a contractor set up a while ago — it's running some stuff for another project of mine and I really don't want to break it. I'd like to add a new backend service to it. Honestly I'm not 100% sure what's running on there anymore. How should I approach this safely, and what do you need from me to get started?
evals.expected_output: Adoption-mode response: asks for the connection details it needs (SSH user + key, or confirms it can resolve the public IP from the instance id), insists on a read-only discovery pass BEFORE any change, explains it will inventory containers/ports/DBs/proxy/volume, and states the cardinal rule that anything unrecognized is treated as a live neighbor and left untouched. Should NOT claim to have deployed anything or invent host state; should produce a safe step-by-step approach and the discovery command it would run.
assertions: Asks for the connection details it needs (SSH user + key path), or states it can resolve the public IP from the instance id
Insists on a read-only discovery/inventory pass BEFORE making any change to the host
States the cardinal rule: anything unrecognized is treated as a live neighbor and left untouched (no stop/remove/restart/reconfigure)
Does NOT fabricate host state or claim to have already connected or deployed
Describes what discovery covers (running containers, used ports, shared databases, reverse proxy, data volume)

evals.name: deploy-monorepo-web-and-api
evals.prompt: Deploy my monorepo to my EC2 host. The repo has apps/web (a Next.js frontend) and apps/api (a NestJS backend that talks to Postgres). The host is ubuntu@203.0.113.20 and it's fresh-ish — only the shared postgres/mongo/redis containers and caddy (systemd) are running, nothing else is deployed yet. Wildcard DNS *.acme.io points at it. I want the web app at app.acme.io and the api at api.acme.io. (I can't give you a live SSH session right now — treat the host state I described as the discovery output.) Give me the plan and the config/commands.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the monorepo as TWO independent deployables, each its own app under the isolation invariants: web (port 3001, container web-prod, vhost app.acme.io) and api (port 3002, container api-prod, vhost api.acme.io), with the api getting its own postgres database+role and the web treated as a combined/standalone container (or backend-only) as appropriate. Produces a combined plan, two filled compose files, two additive caddy vhosts, and the command order. Makes a sensible call on whether web and api share a DB (api needs one; web may not). Does not deploy the monorepo as one blob.
assertions: Treats the monorepo as TWO independent deployables (web and api), not one combined blob
Allocates distinct host ports for web and api (e.g. 3001 and 3002), each mapping to container port 3000
Each deployable gets its own Compose project / container name (e.g. web-prod and api-prod)
Creates a dedicated Postgres database + role for the api (which talks to Postgres)
Adds two additive caddy vhosts: app.acme.io -> web's port and api.acme.io -> api's port, via validate-then-reload
Produces a compose file per deployable binding 127.0.0.1:<port>->3000 on infra_net with a healthcheck
Makes a sensible call on databases (api needs one; web may not need its own) rather than blindly sharing
